title: Atomic Layer Deposition Method for High-Uniformity Thin Film Formation on Semiconductor Substrates
abstract: A method and apparatus for depositing thin films on semiconductor substrates using atomic layer deposition (ALD). The method comprises alternately supplying precursor gases and purge gases to a reaction chamber containing a substrate, wherein each precursor pulse forms a self-limiting monolayer on the substrate surface. The apparatus includes a multi-zone temperature control system for achieving uniform film thickness across the substrate, and a plasma-enhanced activation unit for reducing deposition temperature. The method enables precise control of film thickness at the atomic scale, with applications in gate dielectric layers, barrier films, and electrode formation in advanced semiconductor devices.
keywords: atomic layer deposition,thin film,semiconductor,ALD,plasma enhanced,substrate processing,CVD
